description: A plain, undesigned close-up photograph of a shirtless detained man photographed against a bare light-gray concrete wall, in the manner of a prison intake/booking photo. The man has a shaved head and extensive gang tattoos covering his scalp, forehead, face, neck, and chest, including large stylized lettering across the skull and forehead, script across the cheeks and throat, a tombstone/'R.I.P.' memorial tattoo on the upper left chest, and a prominent '18' numeral tattoo on the right chest (consistent with the 18th Street Gang referenced in the tweet). The image carries no on-image text, no agency seal, no graphic overlays, captions, or design elements; it is a raw photograph rather than a designed meme or poster. While DHS uses it propagandistically in the tweet ('COLD BLOODED KILLERS') to depict gang members held at El Salvador's CECOT facility, the asset itself is an undesigned candid/booking photograph.
